questions.name: Required Field Completeness
questions.evidence: model_details.name='BERT Sentiment Classifier v1.2'; overview populated; licenses=[{identifier:'Apache-2.0'}]; version.name='1.2.0'; model_architecture describes BERT-base with 12 layers, 768 hidden dims, classification head
questions.quality_note: All five required fields present with concrete, non-trivial content.

questions.name: Overview Length Adequacy
questions.evidence: overview (~130 chars) plus documentation block describing architecture and fine-tuning purpose; combined narrative across overview + documentation exceeds 500 chars
questions.quality_note: Two narrative fields together provide adequate context including architecture and task framing.

questions.name: Tag / Keyword Diversity
questions.evidence: tags: ['sentiment-analysis','bert','pytorch','transformers','text-classification','movie-reviews'] (6); pipeline_tag='text-classification'; model_language=['en']
questions.quality_note: Six tags, pipeline_tag, and language all present.

questions.name: Input / Output Specification
questions.evidence: input_format prose + input_format_map [{text:string},{max_length:1024}]; output_format prose + output_format_map [{predicted_class:integer},{probabilities:array[2] float},{confidence:float}]
questions.quality_note: Comprehensive shape/type spec with structured maps.

questions.name: Schema Version Declared
questions.evidence: schema_version: '0.0.2'
questions.quality_note: Recognizable MCT-style version string.

questions.name: Persistent Identifier Present
questions.evidence: references include 'https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805' (arXiv ID); base_model='bert-base-uncased' (HuggingFace Hub resolvable)
questions.quality_note: arXiv reference and HF base_model both qualify as persistent identifiers.

questions.name: Funding & Acknowledgements Completeness
questions.evidence: owners list ML Research Team and Jane Smith with contact emails only; no funding agency, grant ID, or compute facility acknowledged anywhere in the card
questions.quality_note: Funding and acknowledgement information is entirely absent.

questions.name: Ethical & Responsible-AI Documentation
questions.score_label: Ethics present, no explicit bias_* / out_of_scope fields
questions.evidence: considerations.ethical_considerations covers 'Bias in training data' and 'Potential for misuse in censorship' with mitigation strategies; bias_model/bias_output/bias_input fields not used; out_of_scope_uses field absent (limitations field used instead)
questions.quality_note: Ethical considerations are concrete and include mitigations, but the rubric's explicit bias_* and out_of_scope_uses slots are not populated.

questions.name: License Clarity & SPDX Compliance
questions.score_label: SPDX identifier without explicit restriction statement
questions.evidence: licenses: [{identifier: 'Apache-2.0'}]
questions.quality_note: Valid SPDX identifier; no custom_text or explicit restriction statement to reach a perfect 5.

questions.name: Framework / Library Standardization
questions.evidence: framework='PyTorch'; framework_version='2.0.1'; library_name='transformers'; model_index has Papers-with-Code structure (task, dataset, metrics, source)
questions.quality_note: All four signals present and consistent; transformers library version is not pinned separately, kept at 4.

questions.name: Tool & Software Transparency
questions.evidence: framework='PyTorch', framework_version='2.0.1', library_name='transformers'; deployment_info.container_image='example-org/bert-sentiment:1.2.0'; no Dockerfile reference, no usage_documentation.code_examples, no pinned dependency lockfile
questions.quality_note: Several tools and a container image are listed, but reproducibility-relevant pinning (lockfile, full env) is missing.

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure field is absent; no optimizer, learning rate, batch size, epochs, or schedule disclosed anywhere
questions.quality_note: Training procedure is entirely omitted in this harmonized card.

questions.name: Version History Documentation
questions.score_label: Semver name + ISO date + non-trivial diff
questions.evidence: version.name='1.2.0'; version.date='2025-11-20'; version.diff lists three concrete changes (accuracy improvement, longer sequences, negation bias fix)
questions.quality_note: All three version sub-fields populated with meaningful, model-specific content.

questions.name: Citations & References
questions.evidence: citations: 1 APA citation; references: arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805 and example.org/models/bert-sentiment
questions.quality_note: A single APA citation and two references; no BibTeX, only one external citation.

questions.name: Compute Infrastructure & Energy
questions.score_label: Inference hardware listed only; no training compute, no energy
questions.evidence: model_parameters.compute_infrastructure absent; deployment_info.hardware_requirements (4GB RAM, NVIDIA T4, ~50ms latency) describes inference only; no training GPU-hours, FLOPs, or energy/carbon estimate
questions.quality_note: Some inference-side hardware notes exist, but the required compute_infrastructure section and training-compute/energy reporting are absent.

questions.name: Findability (Persistent Landing)
questions.evidence: references include arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805 and example.org/models/bert-sentiment; deployment_info.inference_endpoint also present
questions.quality_note: At least one resolvable landing URL provided.

questions.name: Accessibility & Inference Path
questions.score_label: Format specs + endpoint, no runnable code example
questions.evidence: input_format/output_format prose + input_format_map/output_format_map; deployment_info.inference_endpoint='https://api.example.org/models/bert-sentiment/predict'; no usage_documentation.code_examples block
questions.quality_note: Structured I/O and an API endpoint are documented, but no runnable code snippet or library hook example is provided.

questions.name: Performance Metrics with Slices & CI
questions.score_label: >=2 metrics, >=2 slices, CI on at least one
questions.evidence: 5 metrics: accuracy=0.92 (CI [0.91,0.93]) on test_set; f1=0.91 on test_set_positive; f1=0.93 on test_set_negative; precision=0.90 on test_set_positive; recall=0.94 on test_set_positive; 3 distinct slices
questions.quality_note: Multiple metrics across multiple slices, with a confidence interval on accuracy.

questions.name: Out-of-Scope Uses, Limitations & Tradeoffs
questions.evidence: considerations.limitations: 4 concrete entries (sarcasm, domain shift, English-only, 1024-token cap); considerations.tradeoffs: accuracy vs. speed discussion with DistilBERT alternative; considerations.out_of_scope_uses field not used
questions.quality_note: Two of three target fields are populated with concrete, model-specific content; the explicit out_of_scope_uses slot is missing.

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.evidence: model_index in Papers-with-Code shape; base_model='bert-base-uncased' (HF Hub link); dataset_documentation.training_datasets points to D4D datasheet URLs (imdb-sentiment-v1, sst2-v1) with datasheet_doi='10.18653/v1/P11-1015'
questions.quality_note: Multiple cross-platform interlinks verified: base_model, PwC model_index, and external D4D datasheet references.

strengths: Complete structural completeness across all five Q1-Q5 items (21/21).
Strong cross-platform interlinking via base_model, PwC model_index, and external D4D datasheet references aligned with the harmonized pattern.
Performance reporting includes 5 metrics across 3 slices with a confidence interval on accuracy.
Version history is exemplary: semver, ISO date, and a concrete change diff.
Structured input/output format maps in addition to prose.
weaknesses: No training_procedure section at all - no optimizer, LR, batch size, epochs, or schedule.
No compute_infrastructure or energy/carbon reporting; only inference hardware mentioned.
considerations.out_of_scope_uses and bias_model/bias_output/bias_input slots are not populated; relevant content is folded into limitations and ethical_considerations.
No runnable code example under usage_documentation.code_examples.
recommendations: Add a model_parameters.training_procedure block with optimizer, learning rate, batch size, epochs, scheduler, and regularization to recover Q12 points.
Add model_parameters.compute_infrastructure with training hardware, GPU-hours or FLOPs, and an energy/carbon estimate.
Populate considerations.out_of_scope_uses explicitly (e.g., medical, legal, non-English domains) to lift Q8 and Q19.
Use bias_model/bias_output/bias_input slots to make bias disclosures machine-discoverable, in addition to ethical_considerations narrative.
Add a usage_documentation.code_examples block (e.g., a minimal transformers pipeline snippet) to reach a 5 on Q17.
If applicable, record funding source, grant ID, and compute facility under model_details.contributors[].affiliation or mission_relevance.
